shared_properties: The building occupies a 64x64px footprint sized for the settlement grid and winter overview camera.
Core palette fields remain fixed at primary #111111, background #000000, and accent #FFFFFF.
Roof snow, wall beams, and doorway contrast remain strong enough for rapid structure recognition.
differences: This longhouse sheet represents the default housing unit that supports early population growth.
A 3-frame chimney smoke loop adds life without masking the dark doorway and roof edge.
Snow buildup on the roof ridge signals the harsh winter setting while preserving building readability.

reference_images.label: jarl_of_blizzard_village_scene
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the settlement composition, building spacing, and whiteout layering over the frozen basin.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: TextureAtlas building sprite placed on the settlement grid as a core housing structure with a compact smoke loop.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load world/art_000011.png during building boot and use atlas frame cycling for the hearth smoke motion.
runtime_notes: Keep the footprint centered on a single 64px grid tile so placement remains consistent across the village.
Advance the smoke loop at 3fps to avoid visual noise in the static overview camera.
Reserve the brightest whites for snowcaps and smoke puffs rather than wall surfaces.

context.usage: Core housing building used to communicate population capacity and village growth under winter pressure.

shape.silhouette: Square longhouse with pitched snow roof, dark doorway, and a thin chimney plume.

acceptance_criteria: Sprite sheet is exactly 192x64px with 3 frames of 64x64px and 0px spacing.
Doorway and wall beams remain readable against nearby snow tiles at 1x scale.
Smoke motion stays subtle and never obscures the roof silhouette.
